evals.prompt: XLE is up 4.2% this week while SPY is flat and QQQ is down 1.5%. IWM is down 2%. GLD is up 1.8%. What does this sector rotation pattern tell us, and what credit spread opportunities does it suggest?
evals.expected_output: Sector rotation analysis with relative strength scores, rotation stage identification, and specific credit spread recommendations
expectations: Computes or estimates relative strength ratios (XLE/SPY, QQQ/SPY, IWM/SPY)
Identifies the rotation pattern (risk-off with energy/gold outperforming)
Classifies the economic cycle stage implied by this rotation
Includes Devil's Advocate questioning whether this is a real rotation or a short-term anomaly
Provides specific credit spread suggestions tied to the rotation signal
Checks for false signal indicators (month-end rebalance, OpEx, single-source bias)
Includes an 'If rotation judgment is wrong' section

evals.prompt: SMH (semiconductors) just broke out to a new 52-week high, up 8% in 2 days. The XLF/SPY ratio is at a 6-month high. IWM is keeping pace with SPY. Characterize this sector environment.
evals.expected_output: Risk-on sector analysis with growth/value rotation assessment and credit spread positioning
expectations: Identifies risk-on environment (tech leadership, financial strength)
Notes SMH/QQQ ratio implications for semiconductor-led growth
Assesses whether IWM parity with SPY signals broad participation
Recommends Bull Put Spread candidates in leading sectors
Flags potential false signal risk (single-day breakouts can be faded)
Provides scenario weight implications (base case / expansion scenario favored)
